dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: One night after another you just keep turning up, sister. Are you enjoying this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You got here rather quick tonight, didn't you, witch. Some dark gods aiding your ascent?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Already made it all this way. Some strangeness is afoot... but I care not for that right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Whether we fight together or against each other matters not; only the fight itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Should be more than enough carnage for the both of us here any moment now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Go home, witch. You're no match for my club.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Not too cold for you up here, sister? Or does battle warm you over as it does me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Notice it's harder to breathe up here, sister? Almost like this mountain's cursed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep letting Olympus toss you around, and someday you'll be just like me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You're either good or ill fortune, not yet made up my mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Heracles! What are you doing, fraternizing with our foe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Our foe? Heh. Well you were right again, Prometheus. So then what now? She finally caught us in the act.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: We fight is what, big man; slow her at least. She shall not tell a soul that you were here. The damage it would cause her family would be catastrophic if she did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So then you're both traitors. And you expect me not to notify Lord Zeus and the others? Heracles, he's your father! Yet you would turn on him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't we all? Besides, the Titan of Foresight here knew I would, and who am I to argue? Now allow me and my club to ensure you'll forget what you have seen.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Heracles! What are you doing, fraternizing with him? You're supposed to be defending this mountain like before!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Fraternizing! Heh. Well you were right again, Prometheus. So then what now? She finally caught us in the act.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: We fight is what, big man; slow her at least. She shall not tell a soul that you were here. Her family is in no position to absorb the truth of it, at least not now, or soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know, this sounds a little treasonous. Yet you expect me not to notify Lord Zeus and the others? Heracles, he's your father! Of all the gods, you'd even turn on him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't we all? Besides, the Titan of Foresight here knew I would, and who am I to argue? Now allow me and my club to ensure you'll forget what you have seen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Agent of Change! I of course foresaw my own defeats. As to whether you can stop both Heracles and me, you'll find there are far fewer paths to that outcome.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Is it necessary that you tell this witch such things, Flame Thief? Leave her to figure it out the hard way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew you'd say that, man. And you've the right of it of course; we are not here for conversation. Nor is she.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: If there exists a downside to having the ferocious strength of Heracles at one's call, it must be overconfidence. How could we possibly not prevail, I theorized. Well... there were ways.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You ought have told me of these ways, Flame Thief. For I am unaccustomed to defeat. It's fortunate we have no onlookers, for all the smoke and ash...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you two would like to keep this lovely conversation going, I've no great impulse to break it up. But you're going to force me to fight, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's ultimately why we're here, so yes. Where you're going, Change follows. And Change does not come without conflict.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Heracles! What do you have to say for yourself in all this? Are you the one who broke Prometheus from his bonds?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll get no answers from me, sister; only a splitting headache. You're better off demanding truths from the Titan standing by my side, who speaks them willingly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Truths are only truths if they're believed. And no matter what I tell our adversary, she shall not believe me; not entirely at least... the splitting headache is the better course for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No sign of your big friend. It seems you'll have to contend with me on your own, since I'm ignoring your bird.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My big friend, as you call him, answers to a higher power, and I know that you know what it is. You wish to face the two of us again... you know precisely how.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And why would I possibly wish to go through that again? You're enough on your own, without also having to evade a club twice my size.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're eager to please! To do that which you're called upon to do. That's why you're here. And that's why Heracles shall be here again not very long from now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Aetos has you to himself again! They say that Heracles once slew an entire flock of voracious birds... must make yours a bit uneasy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your insincere concern is meaningless to me. You gods have a shared purpose, merely to reassert your dominance. We share a purpose, too! But one completely different from your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You see, big man? She didn't keep you waiting long at all. Though I know how much you detest having your labors dictated by goddesses or gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I grow accustomed to it more and more. Relieves me of having to make hard choices; I need but make the easy ones, such as which bones to shatter first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: And I look forward to seeing what you decide; though I already know, of course...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We have some matters to discuss, Prometheus! So why not ask your big friend there to step aside? It would be rather rude of him to eavesdrop on our private conversations.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You shall not believe this for some time, Agent of Change, but Heracles can be trusted... certainly not in your present case, but still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I care next to naught for what you say, sister. You wish for me to step aside, make me. Either here and now, or by whichever means you got me to show up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well since the both of you are here, I just want you to know: Neither one of you is as strong as you look.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You underestimate our combined strength, though not for much longer. Let's show her, Heracles!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I may, Heracles? It is not necessary to converse with her like this. We could simply attack without warning.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm only making conversation here because of you. No need to persuade me about the benefits of attack without warning.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Then it's settled! We agree to keep her guessing. Even though we're going to fall back on old habits, with me doing most of the talking henceforth.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You, Heracles! When we meet elsewhere it's as though nothing's changed, and still you face me here? I knew you for a great warrior, but you're just a great actor!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: We're all actors throughout our wretched lives, sister. And don't you dare address me here again; this is the Flame Thief's fight. I'm merely the supporting actor here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: And is this not a fitting stage on which we're to perform before the gods? Including whichever dark gods you yourself invoked, forcing obedient Heracles to answer their call. Let's give them what they desire!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Why don't I take point for a while here, Flame Thief? There are such times as you are liable to talk too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd say I thought you'd never ask, big man, but we both know the truth. I don't foresee the two of you speaking for long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Been so long since I last lost a fight, that I'd forgotten how it felt. The shame... the anger... the blood boiling in my veins.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And it probably doesn't make you feel any better knowing Prometheus there was certain you were going to lose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: No. But something else also occurred: I found myself longing to fight again. To tear my enemies to pieces. I still can suppress the worst part, but I thought all of it was gone...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When first we met, you said you work alone, Heracles sir. Yet here you are cooperating with this Titan, and I'd say even that Eagle too. Another one of your lies, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Who says I'm working, sister? For all you know, this is what I do for sake of recreation. My work and interests coincide.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You expect me to believe you're here because you want to be, and not because the will of Night demanded it? I know you haven't shown up by coincidence alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Believe whatever you like. Now, I believe it's time to drive this club into your skull; not for business, but for pleasure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: It's dark out there, sister. Yet you always seem to find your way, stalking around like a cat. Know what I do to cats?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Apparently you turn them into fashionable pelts! Well you're not doing that to me. How would it even work? I'm too small.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You're too clever. How about you keep talking, while I let my club do the rest of the talking for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever it is you do to get me to show up, tonight you must have done a lot of it, sister. It's crawling out there...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is that a touch of fear in the voice of mighty Heracles? Though I ought to be the one afraid, having to confront you and Prometheus like this. He knows it's going to get ugly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: When does it ever not? But this is nothing. You wish to know true fear, go train under the Centaur Chiron for a while...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I never used to lose. Felt like the Flame Thief here probably does, knowing the outcome of every battle. Not anymore...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Surely the added bit of unexpectedness helps keep things livelier and more entertaining for you now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: No. I never used to lose, but I never liked surprises, either. So, all in all, things lately haven't gone too well for my career...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Of all your kin on this accursed mountain, Lady Athena is the worthiest opponent. My master's legions must be keeping her quite busy, that she'd lend some of her strength to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You call her an opponent but have been avoiding any direct confrontations, throwing your disposable forces against her. You know you'd lose to her in a fair fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would. I lack her martial training, her divinity. But despite her vaunted wisdom, she, like all your kin, possesses what to mortals would be called a fatal flaw. And I know that you know what it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have a brother you never even knew. I had a prideful one, a scatter-minded one, and a pathetic one bearing the weight of the world on his shoulders. Which do you suppose yours is most like?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No wonder you hate my family; you hate yours. Shall we proceed to try and kill each other now? I don't have all night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My brothers and I also used to quarrel frequently. I thought I had outgrown it; become more civilized. But clearly no.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know the tinge of gods, but you have something else. Something older... the mark of Chaos. The eldest, most indifferent force in existence, goaded into action!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not even Chaos wishes to see Chronos prevail. What does that tell you about your own decision to take sides?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It tells me that all beings are fallible, beginning with Chaos. We are all vulgar accidents, do you see? Yet still we strive toward perfection, which remains ever out of reach.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps your so-called master Chronos wants for you to fail... to suffer more than you already have. He seeks vengeance on the gods for overthrowing him before. When you were their ally!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't seek my master's forgiveness, nor assume his loyalty. Alliances are born of shared goals, perhaps ideals; whereas the gods and I are at cross purposes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Because they punished you for crimes you knowingly committed? What else could they have done after you deceived them, and took from them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I took nothing! Fire spreads; burns anything that stands in its path. The gods sought to imprison it, but I set it loose. Now at last, we each are free. And burning still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You work with enchantresses to try and gain the upper hand... but such distractions shall be of no avail against me. It is not the size of the opponent that's of consequence.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To have recognized Madame Circe's craft, only to underestimate its potency. Your foresight evidently has its limits, Titan.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: That it does. But your incantations have their limitations, in kind. You would be wise not to depend on them too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The gods, one of them anyway, extend an offering of peace, Prometheus! Lord Dionysus would have you as a guest of honor at his latest celebration, if you'll indulge him. Given your gift of foresight, you must know I'm entirely sincere.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My foresight only works if there is any possibility of an outcome taking place. Whatever deception you have in mind, you shall have to try harder than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You and your master are different; can you not accept that the gods aren't all aligned in their thinking, either? Dionysus wants no part in your feud!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Dionysus wants merely to drown his few remaining complicated thoughts, and seek more pleasures of the flesh. More beast than god, lacking all self-control.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Dionysus was quite disappointed that you'd not accept the invitation to his feast! Is there truly not some future possibility in which you change your mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: None! I've had my liver shorn apart for aeons by good Aetos here; I'll not permit the god of wine to further damage it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, Titan: Do you recall a mortal woman by the name of Dora? Her Shade responded strangely when I mentioned you, but can't seem to remember what you did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Best leave it be, Agent of Change. That old Shade's latent memories have more to do with gods and their deceptions than with anything I did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Elaborate! Tell me what happened to her. You keep blaming everything on the gods, as though all this is solely their responsibility.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, on the contrary, I take responsibility for mortalkind. But for all their failings, vices, and downfalls, I am not to blame. Nor is this Dora. You tell her I said that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your companion, Dora... she does not remember me, not yet. But she shall! And in so doing, remember why she chose to forget me to begin with... and forget so much more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You say she's not to blame for the failings of mortalkind, as though she could have carried such a burden to begin with. What happened? How do you know her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: That one is haunted by her past, as you can see. But, it is not my place to tell you any more than I already have, lest she recall too much, too quickly. All in due time, as my master would say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You do know Dora, don't you? She and some brother of yours... they were wed? And somehow this resulted in her letting loose all sorts of daemons in the mortal world?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: That is one telling of it, more or less... so she remembers, finally? Perhaps with great regret. After all this time, mortals still cannot decide whether to blame themselves or gods for their plight...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh they should blame only gods? Take no responsibility for their own wretched state? Not even Dora would do that, and she's one of the most complacent beings I know. In an impressive way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your companion Dora remembers some of the events, but this does not mean she understands any of them. Her actions pitted mortalkind against itself forever!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What became of Dora's husband? Your brother, by her recollection. He abandoned her after what happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's more correct to say that your companion Dora finally saw my scatter-minded brother for who he was. Their marriage lasted for about as long as many do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All while you stood idly by and did nothing; but now feel bad and thus decided to shoulder this righteous cause? Perhaps to feel a bit of absolution that you never got from her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your words are harsh but for the most part accurate. Dora remembers now what your own family did to her. If she can forgive you all for that... then she's a stronger soul than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at us, Agent of Change! The daemons your companion Dora inadvertently set free... every ill that could befall mortalkind... most all of them yet plague the gods as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Existence has its hardships. I'm very familiar with many of these daemons, and they are nuisances; but their power has its limits, and they can be driven back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps... but mortals tend to have a harder time of it than you. Don't you see? So long as they suffer, so shall we all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's done...! It's finally done, except... I need to hear you say it first. To know for certain what I have foreseen. Now, please...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You may have to be a little more specific, but... I take it you mean that I found the Fates? Or they found me, but either way. I thought you didn't care for them too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I care for what they say... what they decide to make of this world of ours. What has their exile driven them to do? They would not make the same mistakes again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They've had it with our squabbling; that I can understand. So they have deemed the coming of another age in which the gods apparently shall hold less sway. A time of fewer burdens on us, so our tempers can subside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The coming of a golden age... perhaps not quite the one my master had envisioned, but nevertheless. All this toil, at long last... and till then... I suppose we fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you truly saw all this coming? The Three Fates and their prophecy of a new age. Why have you fought so hard to stop me, then? What if you had prevailed?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew my efforts to prevent you all would be for naught; but I was compelled to make those efforts anyhow. To put it simply, I could see no other way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We might have fought on the same side. Surely that would have sooner led to your result than sending Aetos after me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: A reasonable theory but it never would have gone the way you expect. This was never about mortalkind for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...You're right, it wasn't. I had my own problems to contend with before attempting to rid mortalkind of theirs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yet now you have done both. Fear not; the dead may worship you, but the living couldn't care less. Though still we did what needed to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What of your companion Heracles? I suppose he's been in on your scheme this entire time as well. Appearing to defy the gods while serving them anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll have no more success getting such answers out of me than out of him. You've seen the man; he has his reasons.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hence your affiliation? You must have bonded over your disdain for this mountain and all it stands for. Was he the one who liberated you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: No answers out of me, I said. Not when it comes to him. But at least you now know there are offshoots of your family made of stronger stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Seeing as you know all about the coming age... any interesting details you can share about what's in store?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The world shall be a very different place, in ways you cannot possibly conceive. In other ways, nothing shall change at all; even regress. We shall all be forgotten, then remembered, and forgotten. Again, and again, and again...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The future is a series of fixed points. Not even my master believes this to be true, but I have seen the many possibilities. The only question is... which is our destination?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You fill my mind with this nonsense. Not even the Three Fates know entirely what the future holds, for they are always weaving.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: They were always weaving, yes. I know that you know that they weave no longer now. Yet moment by moment, the future still unfolds. Let us give shape to it ourselves!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Where do you suppose they are, Agent of Change? The Fates, I mean. They, who set the Titans and the gods against each other to begin with, long ago.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wherever they are, pray they can't hear you speaking ill of them. Though why ask me? You're the one who knows everything, including whether they'll ever be located or not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: That I do. I but spur you to consider the possibility, and your place in their scheme; and, I suppose, in mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You, who condemn me as a criminal: Would you know why I liberated fire from this mountain, for the sake of mortalkind?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My own increasingly strong sense of foresight indicates you're going to tell me no matter what.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: When I gave mortals nourishment... the meat they would have sacrificed to you... they needed fire then, and more. But you hoarded all of it! So I saved them; and I'll do it again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why bother talking to me all these nights, Titan? If you know everything I'm going to say. How tedious your life must be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It used to be much worse! But to your point, I would not wish the curse of foresight unto anyone... except perhaps the worst of all my enemies. And you are not yet one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh? I'll have to work harder to earn that distinction, then. And when I set my mind to something, I typically succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am aware. But I also know that pride, one of the defining traits of your kind, tends to precede a fall. You shall see where all your certainty gets you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have questions for me about a great many things... I shall not answer them, not yet. The words must first escape your lips, for one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about I save my breath instead? You won't know what I'm going to say if I never say it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yet your heart if not your eyes and ears suggest to you that I am not the liar that your mind believes me to be. We stand in opposition nonetheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The question you're about to ask is mocking nonsense to supplant the more important subject troubling you now. Go on! I cannot respond if you hold back.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No. You have no control over me or my future. And don't worry, I'm not going to hold back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew you would react this way, but knew no other way to broach the subject. How often I create the outcomes I'm attempting to prevent...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Answer me this one question, Titan. If you know everything the future holds, why make the choices leading to the awful punishment that you endured? Why let yourself get caught?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our choices can reverberate through time, creating outcomes of far greater consequence. If not for my imprisonment, I wouldn't be here now; where I must be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You suffered as you did just so I could beat you up some more tonight? You're a fraud... making unprovable claims to sound righteous and wise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You, of all gods, ought to understand something of sacrifice... but for now, at least, it matters not. I've nothing to prove to you, no secrets I'm willing to share; only my rage.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Either you're a liar when it comes to your supposed foresight, or you're letting me win. Who doesn't want a good thrashing from some witch every so often?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The difference between you and me is that you merely can suspect and theorize, whilst I know for certain. Yet there are truths that I cannot impart.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's quite all right, I didn't think you would. I can change the future with my actions whether I know what the future holds or not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Take it from me, Agent of Change: The more you know of the future, the less likely you are to shape it as you desire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what you're about to say, Agent of Change, and caution you: Don't say it. Should you verbalize your thoughts, the outcome shall be of no benefit to either of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fine... just in case. But you'll forgive me if I'm a little skeptical that we changed the course of history like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You still don't understand. We are as clay, the lot of us. Able to be moulded and re-moulded. One disastrous consequence we avoided; but there's no preventing the next.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Resist all you like, yet the age of gods shall end! Time shall see to it. And thereafter, a Golden Age of enlightenment shall begin, in which you gods hold far less sway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If I somehow gave you the impression that I'm interested in your doomsaying and false prophecies, I assure you, I'm not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your disregard is the very reason you're no longer fit to rule. I know you'll never relinquish this mountain; fall with it, then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear an artifact belonging to one whom I know, I sense it there. How did you get it...? Ah, no need to say; doubtless the gifting custom of Olympus, nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Gifting custom? You mean this Lion Fang from Heracles. What, were you counting on the son of Zeus giving his hard-fought trophy to one such as you instead?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You can be every bit as stubborn and brutish as the beast from which that tooth was torn. Yet you are unworthy of it all the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear an artifact belonging to one whom I know, I sense it there. How did you get it...? Ah, no need to say; doubtless the gifting custom of Olympus, nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean this Lion Fang from your friend Heracles. What, were you counting on the son of Zeus giving his hard-fought trophy to you instead?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You can be every bit as stubborn and brutish as the beast from which that tooth was torn. Yet you are unworthy of it all the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You gods coerced all mortalkind into worshiping you, and for what? You've nothing to gain by slaughtering them at your whim. Leave them be!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll be sure to put in the request with my Lord Uncle Zeus, if you could go ahead and let me past so I can pay him a visit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Lord Uncle had me bound to a mountain for aeons, so that Aetos here could feast upon my flesh for all that time. Aetos came around eventually, but Zeus...? He never shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why do you care so much for mortals, anyhow? Their lives are short, their flesh is weak. Only in death do many of them finally begin to gain some sense.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Any laborer of skill cares for their work. Mortals! Poor sods created in the image of the gods, yet forced to be inferior, so that they could never achieve what my master and I now intend.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean to say that you fashioned mortalkind? Then stole the fire from this mountain for their sake? Absurd.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The truth can be difficult to absorb. Mortals live only to suffer; as I'm responsible for that, I sought to take some of their suffering from them. And redistribute it to all of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Gods could learn something from mortals, you know. For all their many failings, they have an admirable tendency to cling to hope; a certain quality we deathless often lack.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hope can be the salve for the naive. So many mortals hope they'll never die; a feeling born of delusion and fear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes! Mortals live short, painful lives. And even still they strive for something greater than they mostly can achieve. I fight for them! You gods fight only for yourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My master's plan of course requires time. The objective here is not to stop you permanently, but to slow you down.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Unless you just can't help revealing vital information to your enemies, I'm going to assume you're spouting this drivel to mislead me. Either way, my own objective remains the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, bullheadedness is one of your family's defining traits. Then let us execute our respective plans; there is a possibility that mine shall succeed here beyond measure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: When my master lets you cross a boundary beyond this realm to face the monster Typhon as before, the outcome of that, I cannot foresee... how did the last one go?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I'll leave it to your imagination. It must be novel for you not to know the outcome of such incidents.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh that it is. Well then don't tell me, if you like; I'm far more interested in the end result than in the many steps we shall be taking on the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My master indicates that your accomplishments are mounting night by night. He awaits beyond, should you prevail again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: First you, then Typhon, same as ever. Though I'm delighted to know I'm living up to Grandfather's expectations. Could always use more family members to appease.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I do not envy you in that. Nevertheless, so long as our paths are intertwined, I shall be here; no need to let me know ahead of time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: What has the whim of Chaos wrought, that our existence led us to this point, in opposition to each other endlessly?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're welcome to surrender at any point, Titan. I'm certain that Chaos would find it terribly amusing if you did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: As would you, I suppose. But no such outcome ever shall occur, of that you can be certain. Perhaps, however, we may yet provide to Chaos some amusement anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have an avian companion of your own. A scraggly and pallid raven, mediocre wingspan, lacking the raw instinct to hunt and kill like Aetos here. But still, a loyal bird.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And not once has Raki tried to rip my liver out of me. There are much more effective ways to forge bonds with animals, but you must know that now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I was in no position to negotiate with Aetos when first we met. But now he listens to me, typically... although when he identifies his prey, he can become quite fixated by it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems we may be locked here in this struggle for some time; that is the consequence, of course, for what you did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I got my family back. If it means having to get chastised every now and then by you, that's perfectly all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I did not mean it thus. In fact, knowing these meetings may be quite habitual, perhaps it's best we keep our conversations curt. I ought not say too much from here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: This suffocating smoke on a night so black... it fills me with an older strength I thought I'd lost. A good reminder I yet live...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to hear about this positive new turn in your existence, Titan. A fearsome night can certainly instill heightened awareness, and a will to survive.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You speak as though you are the cause yourself; impossible. Or quite unlikely I should say, but then again... augh, you dark gods. Come, let us see whether this is your night or mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've come specifically for me tonight, haven't you? Some barbarous assignment, perhaps, from dark gods far older and greater. Well here I am.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And how could you possibly know? I've said nothing of the sort, Titan. Nor shall I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not this eve you won't, but later. It shall slip your mind that we had this discussion earlier and you shall tell me more. For now, come fight me for the glory of your kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're not my objective, Titan. So what do we say we make this quick? Lest you impede the will of Night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The will of Night, is it... demanding Typhon's sacrifice. Is that what you're after, scaling this mountain again and again?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not necessarily just Typhon's sacrifice. All enemies of the Underworld shall have their punishment. Even you, if you dare keep this up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am no stranger to the punishment of gods. It did not deter me then, nor shall it now, or evermore!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah... so it's to be my borrowed flames against your own this night. This could be rather entertaining if I didn't know already how it's going to play out...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not here for your amusement, Titan. Your stolen flame hasn't made you immune.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Certainly not. And, I anticipate that Ygnium, for I know the weapons that you bear, shall rather sting. But I've felt worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm but a Titan, and unarmed. Should you get past me, however... I know that you know you're certainly no match for what awaits.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you simply know less than you think. And now you're going to call me arrogant or something, I suppose?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would have, yes; but since you've foreseen that outcome, it shall not come to pass. Well if you truly think you can withstand that monstrosity... let's see you withstand me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The monster Typhon isn't liable to stop, you know. He has your family on the back foot, cowering in your Lord Uncle's palace. Exactly where we want them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A suitable distraction I can take advantage of... just as soon as you're out of the way, along with your bird.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're telling me your plans! Now we're truly getting to know each other. I almost regret what's about to transpire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you hear that? Typhon climbs the dark side of the mountain even now. Impressive that you cast him down before... but he shall not rest till the Palace of Zeus lies in ruin.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He'll be destroyed. You along with him if that's what it takes. If he were truly unstoppable, you wouldn't be impeding my ascent. You're buying him time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am. It's strange to think that time can be a limited commodity, even for deathless such as we. But no more stalling for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It may have taken all your godly relatives, but they were able to resist the monster Typhon's wrath, despite the blunders that you made. Yet now you hurry back.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I realize this must be complicated for you, as someone whose loyalties are mostly to an Eagle, but I am not about to let that thing destroy everything my family built.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You ought to choose your loyalties with greater care, not let them be decided by birthright. Well then, come on! You have a summit and a family to protect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Something has changed in you since when last you were here. I know what it is... and what shall happen when you finally make use of it, perhaps even tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You must mean Disintegration of Monstrosity. Yes, I have something for Typhon here. You're fortunate I don't first put it to the test on you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stay your threats! I know you shall not use this power against me; if gods and Titans could kill one another as mortals do, our history would have been much, much shorter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My master does not realize what shall become of him once all is said and done; do you? How abhorrent, to try and steal away one's immortality.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've truly heard it all. A thief, lecturing me about stealing! Chronos took my family, and more... he should realize what shall become of him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's father... you share some of his ancient lineage, and his obsession with the inevitable. Come get what you desire, then! But one outcome awaits as you continue down this path.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been busying yourself elsewhere... down in that Underworld of yours, no doubt. Unless you've started having greater difficulty getting to this point?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're the Titan of Foresight, why don't you tell me? It's a chance to prove this gift of yours is more than a tall claim.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew you were going to say that; so that I could now take this as an opportunity to clarify that foresight is not mind-reading.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It seems the mortals whom you love so much don't all reciprocate, Prometheus. For all you claim to have done, they still don't worship you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor shall they ever, I know. Perhaps make cautionary tales I've inspired but that's it. Fortunately, I don't require affirmation; they can save their Nectar and Ambrosia for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Great Zeus did not have me bound and tortured merely for the supposed crime of taking fire from this mountain. No; he wanted certain information... about his eventual downfall.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you threatened the King of the Olympians with false prophecies and he took it out on you. Perhaps not one of my Lord Uncle's proudest moments, and yet understandable. What did you tell him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I told him nothing! Merely that his time shall come. A specific outcome that grows more likely with each passing night. You are but a wrinkle in the plan, nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I've done my part here, just as you did yours... for this was bound to happen as it did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Curse these old wounds of mine... I once was stronger than this...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I knew that this would be a struggle... but hold always to the chance I may be wrong...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I am beaten. But you cannot afford to give chase, and my strength shall return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The famous wrath of Olympus... it cannot easily be stopped, you see?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Aetos and I have done what needed to be done. Now you go do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If suffering today brings about a better tomorrow, so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I foresaw your trickery yet still could not prevent it, no matter what I tried.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Heh! Compared to what I have withstood before, this was nothing at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If you could see what I've foreseen, you'd not believe it anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You gods never forgive; why then should I, when I could fight instead?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The strength to best both Heracles and me... I can foresee no way to stop it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I have foreseen defeats of mine before... every last one of them, in point of fact...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You have defeated me again... but all my wounds shall heal. They always do...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My strength has failed me repeatedly... but it always comes back...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The flames that you withstood... perhaps they were never meant for you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My foreknowledge of this moment... it's as though... we've done this all before...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Now do you begin to understand... what I have done at every turn...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I knew I could not stop you... on the contrary, you... needed to prevail...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Were it not for this fight... you'd never find a single remnant of Typhon...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... I knew this would happen. Yet I could not prevent it, do you see? I could only... do my part...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Silence. Save your troubles for your master Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not my troubles that I mean to save... Agent of Change...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, here comes the unexpected wrinkle in my master's plan! I know who you are, lost daughter of Hades, and I know that you know who I am. I had a premonition of this very moment.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Prometheus, I presume. You must have premonitions all the time. I'd not heard you were liberated, or that Chronos is the master you serve. Solidarity between Titans, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Call it a change of heart. I was a coward and a fool, you see; to have sided once with gods against my kind. But no more. This is my attempt at atonement.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, by leading this reckless assault? I never took you for a coward, but you remain a fool.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you're right; but I shall be a victorious fool, at least. For I have seen you stand no chance at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you think that you can get past me, if you but continue to try, and to try, and to try. The truth is you shall! Perhaps not this night, but inevitably nonetheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: At least you saved me some suspense. If I knew every outcome as does the infamous Titan of Foresight, perhaps I too would become a disaffected, traitorous wretch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you know of my plight?! You, who flail against Time itself? So much courage born of pride. Come then, Agent of Change, and show me what I know you can do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aetos has excellent vision, of course. Saw you since you first set foot at the base of the mountain. Slowed you down, perhaps, but still... I knew you'd be here now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? And what shall be the outcome of our confrontation this time, since you know everything? Same as the last?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: If you knew, the outcome would invariably be rather different than the one that's imminent. Though I suppose I can at least give you a hint...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew your injuries would be this grave, though perhaps you did not. Nothing a little time can't eventually heal of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm grateful for your concern, though best mind your own injuries. Those bandages... you aren't at your peak. Although perhaps this means we'll have a fairer fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My wounds have long since been a hindrance to me. I do not let them fully heal, so I am always cognizant of what I'm fighting for. And Aetos here appreciates it, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My forces almost stopped you but they didn't quite succeed. Yet even now you're outnumbered against Aetos and me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The prospect of exacting vengeance on your troublesome Eagle, if not you yourself, helps keep me going. Shall we commence?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes. I mustn't keep you waiting. For I know who shall have their vengeance here, and I'm eager for you to find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My forces almost stopped you but they didn't quite succeed. Now you're against Aetos and me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The prospect of exacting vengeance on your troublesome Eagle, if not you yourself, helps keep me going. Shall we commence?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes. I mustn't keep you waiting. For I know who shall have their vengeance here, and I'm eager for you to find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: How can you hope to surpass me in such a wretched state? Perhaps if I were bound in chains and my liver half-eaten this would have been more of an even match.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not under the impression that you seek sporting competition here, Prometheus. Now let's get on with it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: A sporting competition! There must be a certain thrill in not knowing a battle's outcome before it occurs. Even outcomes as likely as this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You cannot die but can be driven back. And you can burn, and shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know the outcome of our each and every clash; yet somehow you surprise me even still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The entirety of my strength shall never again return, but I've enough for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aetos here still longs for the taste of liver, and who are we to deny him that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Agent of Change returns, and the course of history shall never be the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sooner or later, the age of gods shall end, no matter how ferociously you fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm still a little sore after what the gods did to me, for all my so-called crimes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You gods seek worshipers, in all your vanity; we Titans seek only what is ours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I endured having my liver eaten for an aeon; these bouts with you are nothing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The gift of fire I bestowed to mortalkind; I made certain to save some for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you see? Each moment I delay you, Time's plan comes closer to fruition.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whether you shall break me here again is for me to know and for you to find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've seen your every trick; all that you are capable of! And you are not at your best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not be the one to topple this mountain; neither shall you be the one to save it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've seen nothing of this world, yet defend it; I have seen it all, and shall burn it down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You owe your family nothing, yet give everything to them. Fools, all of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: There shall come a time when our conflict has ended; but I wouldn't hold my breath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: What gods and Titans did to one another can never be forgiven... merely forgotten.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: This fire coursing through me, burning me inside... I'll show you how it feels.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I'm already guilty of unforgivable crimes according to Olympus, then this matters not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: No chains shall ever bind me again; I both swear it and I know it to be true.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've some small amount of foresight by now, do you not? You know how this ends.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The gods demand your services and you comply; but I am obligated to resist.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll not attempt to tell you what shall come of this... for you would never believe it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I need no armaments to drive you back... unless you count this flame.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've kept good Heracles there waiting patiently to put that club of his to further use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: We are the fury of Olympus here, not you; and we shall never abandon this fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heracles is bound to serve the gods, as apparently are you. Yet this is the result.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You cannot break us, Agent of Change; but perhaps you can break past us again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I had a premonition of this very moment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know where this all leads. Let's see it through!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I almost regret what's about to transpire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, let us see whether this is your night or mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps it's best we keep our conversations curt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Change does not come without conflict.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems I now pay penance to the gods once more, though they shall never bind me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: How quickly you arrived this night! Bending the very laws of nature to achieve your goal... forging alliances with beings who care nothing for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I did take a shortcut, but you're wrong about the rest. I know the being to whom you refer, and they're not what you presume them to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: No...? But isn't it Chaos that delivers you so close to your goal, yet shall let you go no farther... even if you should prevail here? I know where this all leads; let's see it through!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Agent of Change... the monster Typhon is eliminated and my master certainly is not himself; or rather, now he is. Many have said they knew you could do it but only I truly did. Yet you know as well as I that we're not finished.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos has surrendered, but here you still are, giving his remnant legions their orders. Decided to take matters into your own thieving hands, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: That I decided long ago, when I gave myself to your lord uncle's chains. And I still oppose you, yes. For what you seek beyond, you shall find only through the flames of battle!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The gods are grudgingly permitting this behavior of yours, Titan? The vandalizing of their mountain... the perpetual assaults on their defenses... fighting me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My master, or perhaps my former master as I now should say, wishes to undo certain perceived wrongs, as you are well aware. I merely have a part to play in this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And you're prepared to fight eternally against me, then? For my grandfather to be absolved of the mistakes of his past, and for the future of the gods to be secured?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am prepared to fight eternally for what I believe in; and so that time flows freely forth, if that is what it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The war is over, Titan! Drop the act. You still refer to Chronos as your master, and seem no more charitable towards the gods, despite now doing this for their sake not just yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know as well as I that the conditions of your climb need to approximate your prior path, if you are going to succeed. And I admit that the result I seek would otherwise not be possible. Thus, here we find ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The result you seek involves me repeatedly beating you up? Perhaps all you really do want is a proper thrashing, under the guise of acting like some sort of savior.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have extraordinarily high tolerance for pain. Though for such mockery, less so. Remember, we can both administer a thrashing here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know what lies beyond, and still returned. I knew you would, of course, yet I don't know why. Because you won't tell me...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's right. Your nonsense riddles are a bigger threat to my health than you, or your blasted Eagle pet for that matter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The threat you face is beyond your understanding; that is the only reason you persevere. Your ignorance is a gift! Much like the fire that I shared with mortalkind, and now with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know your capability and can anticipate your every move, but... there are times my mind leads me astray, you see. Anticipating possibilities that do not come to pass... resulting in all too many mistakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mistakes such as revealing this to me, or being here at all. Have you learned nothing from your past imprisonment?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Prometheus_01
dialogueLines.text: To be imprisoned is to be denied the opportunity to learn. A cruel form of torture in itself, but I did learn something, yes: I cannot break.
